decision_rule: Use `event` only when the public record describes a realized downstream adverse effect and the local package already has either direct regulator/court text or an authoritative summary with concrete affected-party consequences.
minimum_public_evidence: Direct regulator/court document text with remedy/action, or authoritative agency summary with named adverse treatment and traceable case/decree context.
hold_or_exclude_if: Do not use when the row is only accusation-stage, only vulnerability-stage, or only a governance concern without a documented downstream adverse effect.
seed_examples: LIMEN-000003 high-confidence anchor; LIMEN-000002 medium-confidence bounded example.
paper_safe_use_now: Methods appendix rule showing that CSET `event` can help interpret selected LIMEN rows without changing LIMEN evidence tier or legal claim ceiling.
visualization_hook: Three-state posture demo where stable event rows are separated from near-miss and hold rows.
next_smallest_move: If complaint/decree text for LIMEN-000002 is captured locally, re-check whether it should remain medium-confidence or become a stronger reusable example.

decision_rule: Use `near-miss` when the public record documents a credible harmful pathway or exploit condition but the local package does not document a realized downstream injury event.
minimum_public_evidence: Authoritative vulnerability/advisory/CVE text or similarly strong technical notice describing exploitability or unsafe execution pathway.
hold_or_exclude_if: Do not use when public evidence already documents realized injury, or when the row is only a vague governance concern without a concrete harmful pathway.
paper_safe_use_now: Security-governance methods paragraph distinguishing credible hazard from realized incident without inflating prevalence claims.
visualization_hook: Security panel contrasting realized event rows with credible-hazard near-miss rows.
next_smallest_move: Test one or two more authoritative security rows before any count or figure treats near-miss as more than an appendix overlay.

decision_rule: Use `issue` when the source documents a serious governance, rights, or compliance-style intervention but the current evidence package does not justify a cleaner realized-event label.
minimum_public_evidence: Direct regulator order/notice or similarly authoritative institutional document showing concern, intervention, or provisional restriction.
hold_or_exclude_if: Do not use as a fallback for accusation-stage fraud or for technical vulnerabilities with concrete exploit paths; those should stay event/near-miss/uncoded depending on evidence.
paper_safe_use_now: Reviewer-safe limitation note showing that some direct regulator actions are more interpretable as `issue` than `event`.
visualization_hook: Methods sidebar explaining why governance-heavy rows should not be collapsed into the same posture bucket as direct injury events.
next_smallest_move: Keep `issue` optional and row-qualified unless a second direct regulator anchor shows the same coder logic cleanly.

decision_rule: Leave the row uncoded when evidence is accusation-stage, document depth is thin, or coder drift would dominate any apparent posture assignment.
minimum_public_evidence: At minimum, public charging-stage or media-summary evidence with unresolved adjudication or incomplete document capture.
hold_or_exclude_if: Hold if the strongest public evidence is only accusation-stage, blocked-text, or summary-level material without normalized underlying document text.
paper_safe_use_now: Strong hostile-reviewer safeguard: demonstrates that LIMEN prefers missing overlay values over unstable posture inflation.
visualization_hook: Appendix row or dashboard legend for uncoded/held cases.
next_smallest_move: If indictment or comparable underlying document text is normalized later, re-evaluate with explicit accusation-stage caveats instead of auto-promoting to `event`.

decision_rule: Treat CSET posture as an optional interpretive overlay, never as a replacement for LIMEN evidence tier, source authority, legality framing, or case truth status.
minimum_public_evidence: Local row already has a LIMEN evidence tier, provenance path, and bounded claim ceiling before any CSET overlay is considered.
hold_or_exclude_if: Do not aggregate posture counts across mixed evidence surfaces or promote the overlay into a headline dashboard metric until multiple row types pass low-drift manual review.
seed_examples: Applies across LIMEN-000001, LIMEN-000002, LIMEN-000003, LIMEN-000005, LIMEN-000006.
paper_safe_use_now: Methods/thesis rule clarifying that external crosswalk presence is interpretive support rather than an evidence-tier override.
visualization_hook: Figure-caption legend or dashboard toggle note rather than a top-line metric.
next_smallest_move: If later lanes add a second reviewer pass, measure agreement before exposing any aggregated posture chart outside appendices.
